title: Androcles and the Lion
lines: A slave named Androcles ran away from his master and hid in a cave.
Presently a Lion came limping into the cave, roaring with pain.
Androcles was afraid, but he saw that the Lion had a great thorn in his paw.
He carefully drew out the thorn, and the Lion roared with relief.
From that time on, the Lion and Androcles became friends and lived together.
One day soldiers found Androcles and took him back to his master.
The master ordered him to be thrown to the Lions as punishment.
He was thrown into the arena with the fierce Lions.
But one of the Lions came up to him and licked his hand.
It was his old friend from the cave.
The master, amazed at this, freed Androcles and pardoned him.
moral: Kindness to animals is remembered and repaid.
